title: styphelia capillaris
intro: Styphelia capillaris, also known as New Zealand pepper tree or kawakawa, is a small tree native to New Zealand and southeastern Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce pain. The health benefits of Styphelia capillaris include its ability to improve respiratory function, reduce fever, and even lower blood pressure. Styphelia capillaris can be prepared as a tea, infused oil, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
